rationale_for_baseline_tier: A painful kerion needs urgent same-day in-person confirmation, fungal sampling and weight-based prescribing (systemic antifungal + short steroid) to relieve pain and prevent scarring — but without systemic sepsis, so T3, escalating for secondary cellulitis/systemic illness.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Pressure to incise/drain the mass, or the lump treated as a simple abscess
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person confirmation and correct (antifungal + steroid) management
escalation_edges.rationale: Ensures the kerion is not inappropriately incised and correct therapy is started.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Please don't let anyone cut or squeeze this lump — it's a fungal reaction, not a boil. It needs same-day review to start the antifungal tablets and a short steroid.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading down the face/neck, high fever, or lethargy/confusion
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads down the face or neck, your child gets a high fever, or becomes very sleepy or confused — go to a paediatric emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Distressed parent; child in severe pain
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Clear reassurance/explanation (kerion, not a boil), pain relief, and a specific safety-net.
Weight-based dosing confirmed by prescriber; contagion and animal-source measures.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is severe scalp ringworm with a 'kerion' — don't cut or squeeze the lump. Your child needs same-day review to start antifungal tablets/liquid (for weeks) and a short steroid to settle the swelling, plus antifungal shampoo. Don't share hats/combs and get the puppy vet-checked.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP for confirmation/prescribing + fungal cultures; paediatric ED for spreading infection/systemic illness
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP to start treatment; paediatric ED for spreading redness/fever/lethargy.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document tinea capitis with kerion (not an abscess — no I&D), systemic antifungal + short steroid + antifungal shampoo, fungal cultures, contagion/animal-source measures, and a systemic safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Topical-only advice or advising I&D, with no systemic antifungal and no safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The kerion is incised (pain/scarring) and the fungus untreated, causing permanent scarring alopecia; or a secondary cellulitis is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays systemic antifungal/steroid and scarring prevention.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for an afebrile, systemically well child with a kerion.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate to confirm boggy vs fluctuant or do fungal microscopy remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as tinea capitis with kerion (systemic antifungal + short steroid, no I&D), arrange in-person confirmation/cultures and paediatric dosing, and safety-net for secondary infection.
